sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Confirmed - reset. No G/g colors. So from Left, only Middle is valid.
Let me now think about this more systematically. I've established:
- Left ↔ Middle: bidirectional connection (both directions work)
- Left → Right: invalid (reset)
- Left → Lower-top: invalid (reset)
- Left → Lower-bottom: invalid (reset)
- Middle → Left: valid
- Middle → Right: invalid (reset)
- Middle → Lower-top: invalid (reset)
- Middle → Lower-bottom: invalid (reset)
- Right → anything: invalid (Right can't even be a starting point)
- Lower-top → anything: invalid
- Lower-bottom → anything: invalid
So the only valid transitions are Left ↔ Middle. This creates a cycle of length 2.
But this doesn't help me solve the level. Let me think about what else could be going on.
Wait, maybe I need to click on the SAME cross multiple times? Like, Left → Left → ...? Or maybe I need to click on the g-marked cells specifically?
Actually, let me reconsider. When I click on Left, the Middle cross gets g markers on its LEFT side (the cells facing Left). When I click on Middle, the Left cross gets g markers on its RIGHT side (the cells facing Middle). These g markers are on the SIDE of the cross that faces the previously-clicked cross.
What if the g markers are actually INDICATING where I should click next? Like, the g markers are on the LEFT side of Middle, meaning I should click on the LEFT side of Middle (which would be the cells between Left and Middle)?
But that doesn't make sense because those cells are just gray grid cells, not crosses.
Hmm, let me try clicking on the g-marked cells themselves. When Left is selected, the g markers are on the LEFT side of Middle cross. Let me click on one of those g cells.
Actually wait, let me re-examine the ascii output more carefully:
Row 18: ...WwwwwWGGNNGGWwNNwWWwggwWWwNNwWWwwwwWWwwwwWBb...
Row 19: ...WwwwwWGNNNNGWNNNNWWgwwgWWNNNNWWwwwwWWwwwwWBb...
Row 20: ...WwwwwWGNNNNGWNNNNWWgwwgWWNNNNWWwwwwWWwwwwWBb...
Row 21: ...WwwwwWGGNNGGWwNNwWWwggwWWwNNwWWwwwwWWwwwwWBb...
When Left is selected (G frame):
- Left cross: G frame (dark gray border)
- Middle cross: has g markers at positions like row 18, cols 25-26 and row 21, cols 25-26 (the left side of the cross)
Wait, looking at row 19: ...WNNNNWWgwwgWWNNNN...
The Middle cross is at cols 23-26 (N cells). The g cells are at cols 27-28 and 30-31, which are to the RIGHT of the Middle cross, not the left.
Hmm, let me re-examine. Actually, looking at the ascii:
Row 19: ...WwwwwWGNNNNGWNNNNWWgwwgWWNNNNWWwwwwWWwwwwWBb...
Let me count the columns more carefully. The grid starts at some column offset. Let me just look at the segmentation to find the exact positions of g cells.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me click on Left to get the g markers, then inspect their positions
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 18}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
